spec: memory > 100MB on n6_atlas 79KB build → retract sparse context tree budget
root_cause: a26_build constructs 6 sparse maps (orders 0..5) with map[ctx]=inner_freq_table semantic; hexa map mutation via tbl[ctx]=inner copies the inner map on each insertion → O(n²) memory accumulation as distinct contexts grow with input length. A26_CTX_LIMIT=65536 cap is dwarfed by per-context inner-map copy cost.
retract: sparse-context-tree budget at A26_CTX_LIMIT=65536 + map-copy semantic
recommendation: reduce A26_CTX_LIMIT (e.g. 4096) AND/OR drop max order to 3 AND/OR reimplement on flat-array freq tables to bypass hexa map-copy O(n²) blowup. Without one of these fixes, A26 cannot reach production-class scale.

spec: PASS 2.5 lookup hit-rate < 10% on text-heavy 1KB → retract sparse-PPM-D-tunes-text hypothesis
interpretation: order-5 hit-rate is saturated (100%) on small text fixtures because 5-byte windows readily repeat in natural English+markdown. Hit-rate gate is NOT the binding constraint — F-A26-4 memory is binding.

caveat: comparison is INTERNAL-ESTIMATOR-vs-WIRE-BYTES (apples vs oranges); A26 estimator does NOT include wire framing overhead. Realistic A26 wire byte estimate = ceil(bits/8) + ~30 bytes header.
